steps.title: Simplify the inequality
steps.content:
Start by subtracting 27 from both sides to isolate the term with $x$:
$$ax - 27 \le 15$$
$$ax \le 42$$

steps.title: The solution form reveals the sign of a
steps.content:
The given solution $x \ge -6$ is a lower bound, not an upper bound. When dividing $ax \le 42$ by $a$, the sign of $a$ determines whether the inequality flips:
  • If $a > 0$: we get $x \le \frac{42}{a}$ (upper bound) — does not match
  • If $a \lt 0$: the inequality flips to $x \ge \frac{42}{a}$ (lower bound) — matches the required form
Therefore, $a$ must be negative.

steps.title: Solve for a from the boundary condition
steps.content:
Since dividing $ax \le 42$ by the negative number $a$ gives $x \ge \frac{42}{a}$, and this must equal $x \ge -6$, the boundaries must be equal:
$$\frac{42}{a} = -6$$
Multiply both sides by $a$:
$$42 = -6a$$
Divide both sides by $-6$:
$$a = \frac{42}{-6} = -7$$
The greatest possible value of $a$ is $-7$.
